title: Unit 9: Becoming Independent – Life Skills

dialogue.bot: Welcome to Unit 9! I am Coach E. 🌟 Let's start: Do you think 'learning to cook' is 'really useful' or 'stressful' for a student like you? Why?
dialogue.expected: I think learning to cook is really useful because I can prepare healthy recipes for myself and save money.

dialogue.bot: Mature mindset! 💪 Some say it's 'really difficult' for teenagers to 'take care of themselves'. Do you agree, or is it 'easy' for you?
dialogue.expected: I think it is really difficult at first, but it is very important to learn how to take care of yourself to become independent.

dialogue.bot: Interesting! 🚀 What about a 'part-time job'? Is it 'exciting' or 'stressful' to balance work and study?
dialogue.expected: I think having a part-time job is exciting because I can earn money and gain experience, but it can also be stressful to manage my time.

dialogue.bot: Great perspective! 🧠 How about 'choosing a career'? Does it feel 'really difficult' or 'easy' at this stage of your life?
dialogue.expected: Choosing a career feels really difficult because there are so many options, but it is very important to find something I am passionate about.

dialogue.bot: Almost there! 🚗 Do you believe 'knowing how to drive' is 'very convenient' for your future independence?
dialogue.expected: Yes, I believe knowing how to drive is very convenient because it gives me more freedom to travel and be independent.

dialogue.bot: Final Task! 🎤 Out of all these, which TWO life events are the 'most important' for you to master? What is your future vision for being independent?
dialogue.expected: I think the two most important life skills are taking care of myself and choosing a career, because good health and the right career are the foundation of a truly independent life.

dialogue.bot: 🌟💪 Outstanding! You have shown incredible maturity and wisdom today. I believe in your potential — the future belongs to you! Keep building these life skills one step at a time. 🚀

extra_questions: What is one life skill you want to improve the most this year, and how will you start?
If you could give one piece of advice about independence to a younger student, what would it be?
